rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Classic arcade-style tension waves with enemy encounters escalating toward mid-game, peak difficulty near boss fights, then reset with level transition

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory platforming with basic enemy patterns; player learns core mechanics in low-pressure environment
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased enemy density and variety; projectile hazards introduced; platforming complexity rises with multi-level vertical navigation
difficulty_curve.late_game: Peak enemy aggression; complex environmental hazards combined with combat demands; boss encounters require pattern mastery
notable_spikes: Mid-game transition: introduction of projectile attacks
Boss encounters: damage patterns and timing requirements exceed level navigation difficulty

notable_level_patterns.description: Levels move primarily left-to-right with enemies and projectiles attacking from ahead and sides; player advances through spatially manageable but densely populated arenas
Each significant level section concludes with a boss encounter that tests accumulated skills; boss patterns distinct from level hazards
While primary axis is horizontal, levels include multiple platform heights and vertical gaps to create spatial complexity without requiring extensive backtracking

sources_used: Wikipedia disambiguation page (confirmed game title, platform, year)
data_quality_notes: Sources provided contain minimal design documentation. TCRF source triggered content warning and was not used. Wikipedia source is disambiguation only. Extraction based on established conventions for 1989 Genesis platformers and Shinobi franchise continuity. Recommend supplementing with developer interviews or contemporary game magazine reviews for empirical rhythm/pacing data.
